intermediate.note: Locked: the Dec 2015 low is independently corroborated by Bullion Trading LLC's LBMA-fix price history (see module docstring for the 3-trading-day date_discrepancy against SLV's own real intraday low), and its degree naming follows elliottwave-forecast.com's own SLV-specific Intermediate-degree count of the decline that ends here -- not subject to promotion. No (III)-equivalent peak label is offered for silver's own real 2011 high, matching GLD's own bootstrap: the cited source characterizes 2011-2015 as a corrective sequence rather than naming 2011 as a specific counterpart turn.

labels.date_discrepancy_note: this source's actual extreme in the search window falls on 2015-12-17, not the commonly-cited 2015-12-14 -- reported as found, not silently corrected to the textbook date

labels.note: start of (V) = the (IV) trough, same locked convention as GLD's own Intermediate (V) (bootstrap_ew_count_gld.py)

primary_label.description: Project-derived hypothesis: a single Minor-degree advance in progress since the 2015-12-17 Intermediate (IV) trough, per the Intermediate (V) framing above. NOT subdivided into Minor 1-5 internal waves here -- same reasoning as GLD's own primary_label: the only pivot detector available ('slow' scale, Intermediate/40wk-18mo degree) does not resolve finer-than-Intermediate structure distinctly, so asserting specific Minor I-V turn dates from it would overstate precision. Left for human review on the deep-history panel. Note this advance includes a sharp, real, still-recent swing (an all-time intraday high near $110 on 2026-01-29 followed by a fast, high-volume pullback into the $50s-$60s by 2026-02, before recovering into the mid-$50s by 2026-07-02) -- a genuine, large intra-advance correction, not a reversal: the 2015 invalidation level below remains unbroken.

alternates.label: elliottwave-forecast.com, "iShares Silver Trust (SLV) Larger Cycles and Elliott Wave": the April 2011 high to December 2015 low decline is a three-swing (corrective) sequence; the bounce into the August 2016 high completed the cycle from the 2011 highs as a large-degree diagonal wave one; silver described as complex/trendless from 2016 onward AS OF that source's own writing
alternates.caveat: An independent forecasting service's own published count, not an institutional consensus -- treated as a candidate hypothesis per §3, not ground truth. Its own "trendless since 2016" characterization predates the real 2024-2026 rally to new all-time highs this repo's own SLV.json shows -- a multi-year complex/sideways stretch inside a longer advance is ordinary EW structure, not a reason to doubt the 2015 anchor itself.

alternates.label: Bullion Trading LLC silver-price history: the LBMA silver price fix bottomed at $13.65 on December 14, 2015 (2015's yearly low), down 18.3% on the year from $16.92 at the start of 2015
alternates.caveat: Factual price/date confirmation (LBMA fix, a different benchmark than SLV's own intraday trading range), not an Elliott Wave source -- corroborates the anchor's approximate date/level, not the degree-naming claim above (that's the elliottwave-forecast citation). Its own Dec 14 fix-low date is 3 trading days earlier than SLV.json's real intraday low of Dec 17 -- see date_discrepancy_note below.

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 2015-12-17 low would invalidate the Intermediate (V)/Minor-in-progress hypothesis.

history_note: SLV.json covers 2006-04-28 to 2026-07-02 (5076 rows) -- real intraday OHLC from its very first row (no close-only prefix). Own-longest-series self-anchor (dashboard_build_spec.md §2: "GLD, SLV, CL, BTC | own longest series"), independent of GLD's own anchor -- silver is bootstrapped separately from gold (same complex, different metal, same as DJI/IXIC each getting their own bootstrap despite both being equities). The fixed Intermediate (IV)/(V) anchor intentionally omits a (III)-equivalent peak label for silver's own real 2011 high -- see degree.intermediate.note.
